column4: Apollo depicted as a savior, healer, and cosmic mediator, paralleling emerging messianic expectations in Judaism and Christianity.
column5: Oracular Apollo as “savior” (soter); Apollo’s prophetic role influences Christian messianic prophecy.

column4: Some Apollo sanctuaries converted to Michaelion churches; Michael inherits roles as healer, protector, and dragon-slayer from Apollo.
column5: Michaelion at Sosthenion (near Byzantium); iconography of St. Michael echoes Apollo’s solar and martial traits.

column4: Apollo’s solar attributes absorbed into depictions of Christ and saints (e.g., halo, radiant cross).
column5: Mosaics and icons; saints as “healers” and “light-bringers” in former Apollo sanctuaries.
